creator: pfeifer@dbai.tuwien.ac.at
text:
On Wed, 14 Jun 2000, Trevor Johnson wrote:
 > I have 4.0-STABLE from May 26.  The port compiled and installed for me
 > with Steve Kargl's change.  However, there was an unrelated runtime error:
 > 
 > 	$ wine
 > 	/usr/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Shared object "libwine.so" not found
 > [...]
 > I was able to eliminate that error.  A look at pkg/PLIST shows that the
 > port is supposed to run ldconfig.  I tried changing a few things in the
 > port, but didn't manage to fix it.
 
 I'm embarrassed. I definitely had tested my patch in PR ports/18877 two or
 even three times on FreeBSD 3.4 boxes before submitting it, but on our new
 FreeBSD 4.0 box I could now duplicate the behavior you're reporting.
 
 The patch below fixes the problem on our FreeBSD 4.0 box. Would you
 mind testing and installing it?
 
 
 Index: pkg/PLIST
 ===================================================================
 RCS file: /home/ncvs/ports/emulators/wine/pkg/PLIST,v
 retrieving revision 1.7
 diff -u -r1.7 PLIST
 --- pkg/PLIST   2000/06/10 05:04:21     1.7
 +++ pkg/PLIST   2000/06/14 18:32:49
 @@ -178,7 +178,7 @@
  lib/wine/libx11drv.so
  lib/wine/libx11drv.so.1
  lib/wine/libwine.so
 -@exec /sbin/ldconfig -m %B
 +@exec /sbin/ldconfig -m %B/lib/wine
  @dirrm include/wine
  @dirrm lib/wine/reg
  @dirrm lib/wine/documentation
 
 
 Gerald
 -- 
 Gerald "Jerry" pfeifer@dbai.tuwien.ac.at http://www.dbai.tuwien.ac.at/~pfeifer/
